prompt: A vast ancient tree photographed from ground level looking up. The lower trunk and roots are covered in cool silver-blue frost and ice crystals, representing the calm pre-industrial era. As the eye travels up the trunk, the bark gradually shifts — warm amber, then burning orange, then urgent screaming red at the canopy. The upper branches are cracked, dry, almost skeletal, glowing ember-red against a dark ashen sky. The transition from cool to burning happens visibly faster in the upper third of the tree — slow change below, violent change above. No text, no labels, no charts, no axes, no thermometers, no globes. Hyper-detailed, abstract artistic style, dramatic chiaroscuro lighting, shot from below with wide angle lens. The feeling of a slow catastrophe suddenly accelerating beyond control.
dataset_name: Global Land and Ocean Average Temperature Anomaly
insight: A sharp and alarming increase in global average temperature after 1980, with the rate of warming visibly accelerating in recent decades.
